fields.name: token
fields.title: Personal Access Token
fields.description: GitHub personal access token (fine-grained or classic)

replication_config.title: Replication Configuration
replication_config.description: Settings for data replication from GitHub.

fields.name: repositories
fields.title: GitHub Repositories
fields.description: List of GitHub organizations/repositories, e.g. `airbytehq/airbyte` for single repository, `airbytehq/*` for all repositories from organization

custom_description:
GitHub is a platform for version control and collaborative software development
using Git. This connector provides access to repositories, branches, commits, issues,
pull requests, reviews, comments, releases, discussions, organizations, teams, and users for
development workflow analysis and project management insights.
supported_questions:
- Show me all open issues in my repositories this month
- List the top 5 repositories I've starred recently
- Analyze the commit trends in my main project over the last quarter
- Find all pull requests created in the past two weeks
- Search for repositories related to machine learning in my organizations
- Compare the number of contributors across my different team projects
- Identify the most active branches in my main repository
- Get details about the most recent releases in my organization
- List all milestones for our current development sprint
- Show me insights about pull request review patterns in our team
- List all unanswered discussions in a repository
- Show me recent discussions in the General category
unsupported_questions:
- Create a new issue in the project repository
- Update the status of this pull request
- Delete an old branch from the repository
- Schedule a team review for this code
- Assign a new label to this issue

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository (username or organization)

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
Optional array of field names to select.
If not provided, uses default fields.

repositories.description:
Search for GitHub repositories using GitHub's powerful search syntax.
Examples: "language:python stars:>1000", "topic:machine-learning", "org:facebook is:public"

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The GraphQL node ID of the comment

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Optional array of field names to select

comments.description:
Gets information about a specific issue comment by its GraphQL node ID.

Note: This endpoint requires a GraphQL node ID (e.g., 'IC_kwDOBZtLds6YWTMj'),
not a numeric database ID. You can obtain node IDs from the Comments_List response,
where each comment includes both 'id' (node ID) and 'databaseId' (numeric ID).

pr_comments.description:
Gets information about a specific pull request comment by its GraphQL node ID.

Note: This endpoint requires a GraphQL node ID (e.g., 'IC_kwDOBZtLds6YWTMj'),
not a numeric database ID. You can obtain node IDs from the PRComments_List response,
where each comment includes both 'id' (node ID) and 'databaseId' (numeric ID).

viewer.description:
Gets information about the currently authenticated user.
This is useful when you don't know the username but need to access
the current user's profile, permissions, or associated resources.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination (from previous response's endCursor)

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Optional array of field names to select

viewer_repositories.description:
Returns a list of repositories owned by the authenticated user.
Unlike Repositories_List which requires a username, this endpoint
automatically lists repositories for the current authenticated user.

Returns a list of GitHub Projects V2 for the specified organization.
Projects V2 are the new project boards that replaced classic projects.

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The file path within the repository (e.g. 'README.md' or 'src/main.py')

parameters.name: ref
parameters.description: The git ref to read from — branch name, tag, or commit SHA. Defaults to 'HEAD' (default branch)

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Optional array of field names to select

file_content.description:
Returns the text content of a file at a specific path and git ref (branch, tag, or commit SHA).
Only works for text files. Binary files will have text as null and isBinary as true.

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The directory path within the repository (e.g. 'src' or 'airbyte-integrations/connectors/source-stripe')

parameters.name: ref
parameters.description: The git ref — branch name, tag, or commit SHA. Defaults to 'HEAD' (default branch)

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Optional array of field names to select

directory_content.description:
Returns a list of files and subdirectories at a specific path in the repository.
Each entry includes the name, type (blob for files, tree for directories), and object ID.
Use this to explore repository structure before reading specific files.
